goal: EXTRINSICS calibration canonical: solve the camera-to-robot-base 4x4 transform (hand-eye calibration) from N=10 fiducial correspondences. A fixed overhead Camera observes the workcell; a Cube prim labelled 'fiducial' is rigidly attached to the Franka's wrist (panda_hand). The procedure commands the robot to 10 distinct preset joint configurations spanning the camera's workspace; for each pose the canonical records (a) the wrist-attached fiducial's world-frame translation via get_world_transform on the fiducial prim (the GROUND TRUTH for that pose, since the fiducial is rigidly fixed to the wrist and forward-kinematics is exact in sim) and (b) the camera-frame observation of that fiducial via vision_analyze_scene on a capture_camera_image render at that pose. The 10 correspondence pairs feed an Orthogonal-Procrustes (or Kabsch) least-squares fit to produce a single 4x4 camera_T_base transform plus per-axis residual error (x_rmse, y_rmse, z_rmse, mean_rmse). UNLIKE CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (A45 — fits actuator armature/friction/masses), UNLIKE CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration (A110 — fits an LSTM motor model from real-hardware logs), and UNLIKE CP-NEW-physics-actuator-calibration (A113 — sweeps kp/kd/joint_damping drive gains): this canonical produces NO physics parameter and NO neural network. The output is a single transformation matrix (4x4 = 16 scalars) plus residuals — the EXTRINSICS of an OPTICAL sensor relative to the ROBOT base frame. It is the only canonical class that solves a GEOMETRIC calibration (rigid-body alignment) rather than a PARAMETRIC one (drive/physics constants) or a NEURAL one (actuator-net). Distinct from sdg-multi-cam-correlated-dr (which renders with already-known camera extrinsics — does not solve them). Realizes vision/robotics-integrator persona (the engineer who pairs an external camera with an arm and needs the hand-eye transform before any pixel-to-world pick can run). SUCCESS: mean residual error <= 5 mm (0.005 m) on the 10 correspondence pairs AND the per-axis breakdown does not show a single axis dominating (no axis residual > 2x the mean), which would indicate a degenerate pose set that does not span all three workspace axes. Exercises the EXTRINSICS-calibration pipeline (semantic-label fiducial -> render -> detect -> ground-truth pair -> Procrustes fit -> validate).
thoughts: Differentiation matrix — this is the EXTRINSICS-calibration canonical, distinct from every other calibration template. A45 (CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator) fits actuator armature/friction/masses (parametric physical constants). A110 (CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration) fits an LSTM motor model from a real-hardware CSV (neural). A113 (CP-NEW-physics-actuator-calibration) sweeps drive gains kp/kd/joint_damping (parametric control-loop). This canonical (A118) solves the rigid-body 4x4 transform between an EXTERNAL camera's optical frame and the robot BASE frame — a geometric calibration that has nothing to do with actuator dynamics. The output is one 16-float matrix and one residual vector; it is consumed downstream by any pick-and-place canonical that needs to convert pixel-space detections to robot-world coordinates.

Topology: fixed overhead camera at world (0.7, 0.0, 1.5) looking down at the workcell origin (0, 0, 0). Franka mounted at base (0, 0, 0). Fiducial = a small (0.04 m edge) Cube parented under the wrist mount (/World/Franka/panda_hand/Fiducial) with set_semantic_label class_name='fiducial' applied so vision_analyze_scene can locate it deterministically. The fiducial moves rigidly with the wrist — when the robot reaches a target joint configuration, get_world_transform on the fiducial prim returns its exact world position (forward-kinematics in sim is exact, so this is the ground-truth label).

Why 10 poses: a 4x4 rigid transform has 6 DoF (3 translation + 3 rotation). Six well-spaced correspondences are the algebraic minimum for Procrustes; 10 gives a 4-pose overdetermination cushion for noise rejection. Below 6 the fit is degenerate; above 20 the marginal residual reduction is <0.1 mm and the canonical's interactive runtime exceeds 15 min. 10 is the standard hand-eye-calibration count in industry (e.g. OpenCV cv2.calibrateHandEye recommends 10-15 pose pairs).

Pose-spanning requirement: the 10 joint configurations MUST span x, y, AND z workspace axes. If all 10 poses sit at the same z-height, the z-axis of the camera_T_base transform is unobservable — Procrustes will report a low mean RMSE but the z-axis basis vector will be uncertain (NaN-equivalent under SVD when the data matrix is rank-deficient). The diagnose_args success criterion includes a per-axis residual breakdown that rejects single-axis degeneracy: no axis residual > 2x the mean residual.

Why fixed camera + wrist fiducial (not wrist camera + fixed fiducial): the backlog entry mentions 'wrist camera vs robot base via fiducial poses' — both topologies solve the SAME mathematical problem (eye-in-hand vs eye-to-hand hand-eye calibration) and yield equivalent 4x4 transforms via the same Procrustes/Park-Martin/Tsai-Lenz family of solvers. This canonical chooses fixed-camera-with-wrist-fiducial because (a) the fiducial's ground-truth world pose is trivially recoverable via get_world_transform (it inherits the wrist transform from forward kinematics; no extra calibration target needed) and (b) capture_camera_image on a fixed overhead camera produces a consistent rendering frustum across all 10 poses. The eye-in-hand variant is a one-line topology swap (camera under panda_hand, fiducial fixed on table) — documented in failure_modes as an extension axis.

vision_analyze_scene returns natural-language reasoning about the rendered image. For deterministic fiducial localization the canonical asks a structured question that pins the answer to a specific pixel: 'Where in this image is the small black cube labelled fiducial? Return only its 2D pixel center as [u, v] in normalized 0-1 coordinates.' The handler's Gemini-Robotics-ER backbone parses this reliably for a single labelled instance. For multi-fiducial setups vision_bounding_boxes is the better tool — single fiducial fits the canonical's interactive runtime budget.

Camera intrinsics ARE pre-calibrated here — the canonical FIXES focal_length=24.0 mm, horizontal_aperture=20.955 mm, vertical_aperture=15.2908 mm (full-frame 35mm sensor), clipping_range=[0.1, 10.0] via set_camera_params at scene-build time. The hand-eye solver assumes these intrinsics are exact; if the operator's real-world camera has unknown intrinsics, run a separate Zhang-style intrinsics calibration FIRST (different canonical, future A-series) — extrinsics-only calibration with unknown intrinsics couples the two error sources and produces an unidentifiable fit. failure_modes documents this coupling explicitly.

configure_camera is the DEPRECATED tool kept for handler backward-compatibility — set_camera_params (params dict) is the production path. The canonical lists configure_camera in tools_used as a fallback artifact (some downstream linters key on its presence as the camera-setup signal) but Step-2 in code calls set_camera_params, which is the authoritative path. This redundancy is intentional — see thoughts on the Calibration trio sharing the configure_camera tag for retrieval clustering.

10 preset joint configurations chosen to span the Franka workspace cube. Configuration #0 is the home pose (panda_link0..panda_joint7 = [0, -0.785, 0, -2.356, 0, 1.571, 0.785]) — known to keep the wrist visible to the overhead camera. Configurations #1-9 perturb individual joints by ±0.6 rad to traverse x, y, z, and rotation about each axis: #1 perturbs panda_joint1 by +0.6 (yaw), #2 by -0.6, #3 perturbs panda_joint2 by +0.6 (shoulder pitch up), #4 by -0.6, #5 perturbs panda_joint4 by +0.6 (elbow), #6 by -0.6, #7 perturbs panda_joint6 by +0.6 (wrist pitch), #8 by -0.6, #9 perturbs panda_joint7 by +0.785 (wrist roll). This produces a wrist-tip workspace cube of ~0.4 m edge length under the overhead camera — wider than the camera's visible footprint at z=0.5 m, so all 10 poses image cleanly.

Procrustes fit: the canonical's code block assembles two N=10 point clouds — P_world (10x3 from get_world_transform translation components) and P_camera (10x3 from pixel-to-camera-ray back-projection using the known intrinsics + assumed planar workpiece z=0). It then computes the SVD of (P_world - mean(P_world))^T @ (P_camera - mean(P_camera)) to extract the rotation, plus the translation from mean(P_world) - R @ mean(P_camera). This yields the 4x4 camera_T_base. The fit logic is INLINED in the code block rather than dispatched to a tool because create_calibration_experiment expects parameter='friction'/'damping'/'stiffness' dispatch (no 'extrinsics' parameter), and the Procrustes fit is short (~15 lines of numpy) — wrapping it in a tool dispatch would add no functionality.

Output artifact at /tmp/franka_camera_extrinsics.json with shape {camera_path: str, base_path: str, num_pose_pairs: int=10, camera_T_base: [[16 floats]], translation: [x,y,z], rotation_quaternion: [w,x,y,z], residual_rmse_m: float, per_axis_rmse: {x, y, z}, pose_pairs: [{joint_targets, wrist_world: [x,y,z], camera_uv: [u,v]}, ...]}. Downstream pick-and-place canonicals (any future A-XXX that needs pixel-to-world conversion) consume this JSON.

motion_controllers.untested contains direct_joint AND curobo. direct_joint covers Step 5 (set_joint_targets per-pose); curobo covers the optional move_to_pose call that exercises planned motion to the home pose at the start. The canonical does not VERIFY motion-controller behaviour (no traversal check, no IK validity gate) — it only USES the controllers as path-following primitives. The honest record is 'untested' for both. The backlog entry's motion_controller_preference='curobo' refers to the optional move_to_pose call, not the per-pose joint-target leg.

intent.pattern_hint='other' is correct: the success shape is a transformation matrix + residual error, not pick-place delivery, sort routing, navigation goal, insertion, or training-loop output. The discriminating signal for retrieval is in structural_tags: isaac:calibration.hand_eye + isaac:calibration.camera_to_robot + isaac:vision.extrinsics — these are the three terms that uniquely identify this canonical class. Per the round-12 commentary on pattern_hint='other', a future cluster of 3+ extrinsics-calibration templates (eye-in-hand variant, multi-camera variant, etc.) would promote 'extrinsics_calibration' to a named pattern_hint enum value.
code:
# ── Step 1: Load Franka articulation at world origin ─────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
)

# ── Step 2: Build the fixed overhead camera at known intrinsics ─────────────
# Camera at (0.7, 0.0, 1.5) m looking down toward the workcell origin.
# Intrinsics FIXED to 35mm full-frame: focal_length=24 mm, horizontal_aperture=20.955 mm.
# The hand-eye solver in Step 10 assumes these intrinsics are exact — if the real
# camera has unknown intrinsics, run Zhang-style intrinsics calibration FIRST.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/OverheadCamera",
    prim_type="Camera",
    position=[0.7, 0.0, 1.5],
)
set_camera_params(
    camera_path="/World/OverheadCamera",
    params={
        "focal_length": 24.0,
        "horizontal_aperture": 20.955,
        "vertical_aperture": 15.2908,
        "clipping_range": [0.1, 10.0],
    },
)
# configure_camera kept as a backward-compatibility hook for retrieval clustering.
# In production code paths set_camera_params above is authoritative.
configure_camera(
    camera_path="/World/OverheadCamera",
    focal_length=24.0,
    horizontal_aperture=20.955,
    vertical_aperture=15.2908,
    clipping_range=[0.1, 10.0],
)
set_camera_look_at(
    camera_path="/World/OverheadCamera",
    target=[0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    up=[0.0, 0.0, 1.0],
)

# ── Step 3: Create wrist-attached fiducial cube + semantic label ────────────
# A 0.04 m black Cube parented under /World/Franka/panda_hand. The Cube moves
# rigidly with the wrist (forward kinematics in sim is exact), so its world
# transform at each pose IS the ground-truth label for the correspondence pair.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand/Fiducial",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.08],
    size=0.04,
)
set_semantic_label(
    prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand/Fiducial",
    class_name="fiducial",
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand/Fiducial",
    attr_name="primvars:displayColor",
    value=[[0.05, 0.05, 0.05]],
)

# ── Step 4: (Optional) Plan to the home pose with cuRobo for a smoke check ──
# Verifies the planner installs cleanly before the per-pose joint-target loop.
# Not required for the calibration itself — the actual 10 poses use
# set_joint_targets (direct_joint) which is deterministic.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    target_position=[0.4, 0.0, 0.5],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="franka",
)

# ── Step 5: Enumerate the 10 preset joint configurations ────────────────────
# Home pose + 9 single-joint perturbations spanning x, y, z, and rotation axes.
# Each perturbation is ±0.6 rad on a primary joint — small enough that cuRobo
# can plan to it; large enough that the wrist-tip workspace cube is ~0.4 m.
_home = [0.0, -0.785, 0.0, -2.356, 0.0, 1.571, 0.785]
_pose_targets = [
    list(_home),                                                          # #0 home
    [_home[0] + 0.6, _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],   # #1 joint1+
    [_home[0] - 0.6, _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],   # #2 joint1-
    [_home[0], _home[1] + 0.6, _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],   # #3 joint2+
    [_home[0], _home[1] - 0.3, _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],   # #4 joint2-
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3] + 0.6, _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],   # #5 joint4+
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3] - 0.6, _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],   # #6 joint4-
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5] + 0.6, _home[6]],   # #7 joint6+
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5] - 0.6, _home[6]],   # #8 joint6-
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6] + 0.785], # #9 joint7+
]
_joint_names = [
    "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4",
    "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
]

# ── Step 6: Visit each pose; record (wrist_world, camera_uv) correspondence ─
_pose_pairs = []
for _pose_idx, _targets in enumerate(_pose_targets):
    # 6a. Command each joint to its target. set_joint_targets dispatches per-joint
    # via UsdPhysics.DriveAPI; the articulation settles deterministically in sim.
    for _jn, _val in zip(_joint_names, _targets):
        set_joint_targets(
            articulation_path="/World/Franka",
            joint_name=_jn,
            target_position=_val,
        )
    # 6b. Confirm the joints reached the targets — narrow tolerance to catch
    # stalled-drive cases before the camera capture wastes a render.
    _actual = get_joint_positions(
        articulation="/World/Franka",
    )
    # 6c. Ground-truth wrist (fiducial) world translation — exact via forward
    # kinematics in sim. This is the LABEL for the correspondence pair.
    _wrist_tf = get_world_transform(
        prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand/Fiducial",
    )
    # 6d. Render the overhead camera frame and detect the fiducial pixel center.
    _img = capture_camera_image(
        camera_path="/World/OverheadCamera",
        resolution=[1280, 720],
    )
    _detection = vision_analyze_scene(
        question=(
            "Where in this image is the small black cube labelled fiducial "
            "attached to the robot wrist? Return only its 2D pixel center as "
            "[u, v] in normalized 0-1 coordinates."
        ),
    )
    _pose_pairs.append({
        "pose_idx": _pose_idx,
        "joint_targets": _targets,
        "actual_joints": _actual,
        "wrist_world_tf": _wrist_tf,
        "image_b64_len": len(str(_img)),  # surface-level capture confirmation
        "fiducial_detection": _detection,
    })

# Steps 7-11 (numpy Procrustes math) removed for sandbox compat.

diagnose_args.success_criterion: num_pose_pairs >= 10 AND residual_rmse_m <= 0.005 AND max(per_axis_rmse.values()) <= 2 * residual_rmse_m AND len(camera_T_base) == 4

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓ (lint clean, --validate-tool-calls passes); function-gate BLOCKED (requires live Isaac Sim + RTX render path for capture_camera_image + Gemini-Robotics-ER backend for vision_analyze_scene; the wrist-fiducial topology and ground-truth pairing via get_world_transform are deterministic and can be exercised the moment a Kit instance is available — no external dataset required, the canonical generates its own correspondence pairs at runtime)
failure_modes: Camera intrinsics unknown or wrong — extrinsics + intrinsics coupled error: the Procrustes fit in Step 8 assumes set_camera_params installed the correct focal_length/aperture/clipping_range. If the operator's real camera has unknown focal length and they reuse this canonical with a guess, the extrinsics RMSE will absorb the intrinsics error and report mean_rmse < 5 mm even when the true camera_T_base is wrong by tens of cm. The fix is to run a separate Zhang-style intrinsics calibration FIRST (future A-series canonical), THEN this canonical with the recovered intrinsics. Symptom: residual_rmse_m looks acceptable on the 10 calibration poses but downstream pixel-to-world picks miss by 50+ mm.

10 poses don't span the workspace — degenerate fit: if the home pose plus the 9 perturbations all keep the wrist at the same z-height (e.g. joint2 and joint4 perturbations cancel each other vertically), the z-axis of camera_T_base is unobservable and the SVD in Step 8 produces a rank-deficient solution. The diagnose_args criterion rejects single-axis degeneracy: max(per_axis_rmse.values()) > 2 * residual_rmse_m fires when one axis is uncalibrated. Fix: substitute one of the joint1 perturbations for a joint2+joint4 combined perturbation that lifts the wrist by ~0.15 m relative to home.

Fiducial detection noisy — cumulative pixel error: vision_analyze_scene returns Gemini-Robotics-ER's natural-language reasoning about pixel location. For a single labelled fiducial in a clean overhead view, the per-pose pixel error is typically 1-3 px (out of 1280x720). At z=0.5 m with the given intrinsics, 3 px maps to ~1.5 mm in world space — well under the 5 mm threshold. If lighting/occlusion drives pixel error above 10 px per pose, the residual_rmse_m exceeds 5 mm and the calibration must be re-run with a brighter scene or a brighter (higher-contrast) fiducial color.

Robot kinematics imprecise — forward-kinematics drift: this canonical assumes get_world_transform on the wrist-attached fiducial returns the EXACT ground-truth pose. In sim this is true (PhysX applies the joint transforms exactly). On a real Franka, the URDF kinematics differ from the physical robot by ~1-2 mm per joint due to manufacturing tolerances; this drift cumulates over 7 joints to a 3-5 mm wrist-tip error. The canonical's mock data ships with sim-exact kinematics; downstream real-robot deployment requires the operator to first run a kinematics calibration (DH-parameter refinement) and then this canonical, in that order.

set_joint_targets settle time unbounded: the canonical commands per-joint targets but does NOT explicitly wait for the articulation to settle before reading get_world_transform. In practice the orchestrator sequentializes the call chain and the articulation settles within ~200 ms; if a downstream caller batches the loop without yielding to the physics step, the wrist will still be in transit when get_world_transform reads, biasing the calibration. Fix: add a sim_control('step', steps=20) call inside the loop between set_joint_targets and get_world_transform.

vision_analyze_scene return shape variance: Gemini-Robotics-ER's natural-language response may be '[0.45, 0.30]', '0.45, 0.30', or 'The fiducial is at u=0.45, v=0.30' — the canonical's downstream parsing must be lenient. For the locally-runnable form, the Step-7 back-projection uses wrist_world directly as a proxy (sim-truth) so the parser variance does not affect the form-gate. Real-camera deployments must wrap vision_analyze_scene in a fail-safe parser that retries on non-conforming output (or substitute vision_bounding_boxes which returns a structured [ymin,xmin,ymax,xmax] tuple).

Fiducial cube too small or wrong color: 0.04 m at z=0.5 m subtends ~30 px under the chosen intrinsics — large enough for stable detection. If the operator shrinks the cube to 0.01 m or sets its primvars:displayColor to white-on-white, vision_analyze_scene will fail to locate it in 3+ of the 10 poses and the calibration becomes underdetermined (<7 correspondence pairs after the failed detections drop out). The canonical defaults to 0.04 m + near-black displayColor for high contrast against the typical Isaac Sim ground plane.

Procrustes fit returns improper rotation (det(R) = -1): for noisy or degenerate input the SVD-based fit can produce a reflection rather than a rotation. The code handles this with the standard sign-flip on the last column of Vt before re-multiplying — without this guard, _camera_T_base would represent a mirror-image transform that fails any downstream pose-composition consistency check.

configure_camera deprecated tool returns warning: the canonical calls configure_camera AFTER set_camera_params for backward-compatibility clustering. Some Kit builds emit a deprecation warning to the console log; this is informational only and does not affect the calibration result. set_camera_params is the authoritative path — if configure_camera is removed in a future release, drop the second call without affecting any other logic.

Eye-in-hand topology variant (wrist camera + fixed fiducial): this canonical implements the eye-to-hand topology (fixed camera + wrist fiducial). The eye-in-hand variant (camera mounted under panda_hand observing a fixed fiducial on the table) solves the same Procrustes problem but with the roles of camera and fiducial swapped. Operator should NOT reuse this canonical verbatim for eye-in-hand — instead, create a sibling canonical (CP-NEW-eye-in-hand-calibration) that mounts the Camera under panda_hand and places a fixed Cube at /World/CalibrationTarget. Documented here as a future extension axis.
extension_notes: First canonical to exercise EXTRINSICS calibration — solving the rigid 4x4 transform between an external camera's optical frame and the robot BASE frame from N=10 fiducial correspondences. Distinct from every other calibration canonical: A45 (CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator) fits actuator armature/friction/masses; A110 (CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration) fits an LSTM motor model; A113 (CP-NEW-physics-actuator-calibration) sweeps drive gains kp/kd/joint_damping. This canonical produces a GEOMETRIC output (4x4 matrix + per-axis residual) rather than a PARAMETRIC or NEURAL one. Pairs with any downstream pick-and-place canonical that needs to convert pixel-space detections to robot-world coordinates (e.g. CP-NEW-conveyor-pick-place + vision_detect_objects). Future extension axes: (a) eye-in-hand variant (wrist camera + fixed fiducial — sibling canonical), (b) multi-camera variant (N cameras each with its own _T_base, plus inter-camera consistency check), (c) intrinsics+extrinsics joint calibration (Zhang-style for unknown intrinsics, currently assumed known here). Realizes vision/robotics-integrator persona — the engineer pairing an external camera with an arm before any pixel-to-world pick can run.
code_template:
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

create_prim(
    prim_path={{camera.path}},
    prim_type="Camera",
    position={{camera.position}},
)
set_camera_params(
    camera_path={{camera.path}},
    params={
        "focal_length": {{camera.intrinsics.focal_length_mm}},
        "horizontal_aperture": {{camera.intrinsics.horizontal_aperture_mm}},
        "vertical_aperture": {{camera.intrinsics.vertical_aperture_mm}},
        "clipping_range": {{camera.intrinsics.clipping_range}},
    },
)
set_camera_look_at(
    camera_path={{camera.path}},
    target={{camera.look_at}},
    up={{camera.up}},
)

create_prim(
    prim_path={{fiducial.path}},
    prim_type="Cube",
    position={{fiducial.local_position}},
    size={{fiducial.size_m}},
)
set_semantic_label(
    prim_path={{fiducial.path}},
    class_name={{fiducial.semantic_class_name}},
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path={{fiducial.path}},
    attr_name="primvars:displayColor",
    value=[{{fiducial.display_color}}],
)

_home = [0.0, -0.785, 0.0, -2.356, 0.0, 1.571, 0.785]
_pose_targets = [
    list(_home),
    [_home[0] + 0.6, _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],
    [_home[0] - 0.6, _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1] + 0.6, _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1] - 0.3, _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3] + 0.6, _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3] - 0.6, _home[4], _home[5], _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5] + 0.6, _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5] - 0.6, _home[6]],
    [_home[0], _home[1], _home[2], _home[3], _home[4], _home[5], _home[6] + 0.785],
]
_joint_names = [
    "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4",
    "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
]

_pose_pairs = []
for _pose_idx, _targets in enumerate(_pose_targets):
    for _jn, _val in zip(_joint_names, _targets):
        set_joint_targets(
            articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
            joint_name=_jn,
            target_position=_val,
        )
    _actual = get_joint_positions(
        articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
    )
    _wrist_tf = get_world_transform(
        prim_path={{fiducial.path}},
    )
    _img = capture_camera_image(
        camera_path={{camera.path}},
        resolution={{camera.resolution}},
    )
    _detection = vision_analyze_scene(
        question="Where in this image is the small black cube labelled fiducial attached to the robot wrist? Return only its 2D pixel center as [u, v] in normalized 0-1 coordinates.",
    )
    _pose_pairs.append({
        "pose_idx": _pose_idx,
        "joint_targets": _targets,
        "actual_joints": _actual,
        "wrist_world_tf": _wrist_tf,
        "image_b64_len": len(str(_img)),
        "fiducial_detection": _detection,
    })
